findings.evidence: Lines 62-69 add `hinvolp`, line 93 is its only use, and Mathlib defines `HeightOneSpectrum.equivOfRingEquiv` as an equivalence at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/DedekindDomain/Ideal/Lemmas.lean:596`, so injectivity gives the needed equality directly.
findings.fix: Drop `hinvolp` from the theorem signature and call at line 157; in the `σ x = q` branch derive `x = p` from `h : σ x = q` after rewriting `hqdef` and using injectivity of the equivalence.
findings.issue: `equivOfRingEquiv_notMem_pair` assumes `hinvolp : σ (σ p) = p`, but the second branch only needs injectivity of `HeightOneSpectrum.equivOfRingEquiv σ` together with `hqdef : q = σ p`; the extracted lemma is stronger than the argument requires.

summary: The extracted helper adds an unnecessarily strong local hypothesis. Remove the unused pointwise involution assumption before landing the cleanup.
